User.title: User
User.description: Represents an administrative user with access to the ChemGuard AI dashboard. Does not store authentication credentials, which are handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

email.description: The email address of the user.

role.description: The role of the user within the system (e.g., 'Admin', 'Dispatcher', 'Monitor').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

lastLoginAt.description: Timestamp of the user's last login.

Driver.title: Driver
Driver.description: Represents a vehicle driver responsible for shipments.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Driver entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the driver.

lastName.description: The last name of the driver.

contactNumber.description: The primary contact number for the driver.

currentVehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle currently assigned to this driver. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:1 Driver)

status.description: Current operational status of the driver (e.g., 'Available', 'On Duty', 'Off Duty').

Location.title: Location
Location.description: Represents a geographical point or address used for origins, destinations, checkpoints, and event locations.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Location entity.

name.description: A common name for the location (e.g., 'Warehouse A', 'Port of Rotterdam').

address.description: The full street address of the location.

state.description: The state or province of the location.

postalCode.description: The postal code of the location.

latitude.description: The geographical latitude of the location.

longitude.description: The geographical longitude of the location.

Vehicle.title: Vehicle
Vehicle.description: Represents a transport vehicle (e.g., truck) used for shipments, including its status and assigned driver.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Vehicle entity.

year.description: The manufacturing year of the vehicle.

driverId.description: Reference to the Driver currently operating this vehicle. (Relationship: Driver 1:1 Vehicle)

currentLocation.description: Current geographical coordinates of the vehicle (e.g., 'latitude,longitude').

locationDescription.description: A human-readable description of the vehicle's current location.

healthStatus.description: The overall health status of the vehicle (e.g., 'Good', 'Warning', 'Critical').

movementStatus.description: Current movement status of the vehicle (e.g., 'Moving', 'Stopped', 'Idle').

lastReportedAt.description: Timestamp when the vehicle's status or location was last reported.

iotDeviceIds.description: References to IoTDevice entities installed on this vehicle. (Relationship: IoTDevice N:N Vehicle)

Shipment.title: Shipment
Shipment.description: Represents a single logistical movement of chemical batches from an origin to a destination.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Shipment entity.

status.description: Current status of the shipment (e.g., 'Scheduled', 'In Transit', 'Delayed', 'Delivered', 'Cancelled').

originLocationId.description: Reference to the Location entity where the shipment originated. (Relationship: Location 1:N Shipment)

destinationLocationId.description: Reference to the Location entity where the shipment is destined. (Relationship: Location 1:N Shipment)

currentLocation.description: Current geographical coordinates of the shipment (e.g., 'latitude,longitude').

locationDescription.description: A human-readable description of the shipment's current location.

vehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle assigned to this shipment. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N Shipment)

driverId.description: Reference to the Driver responsible for this shipment. (Relationship: Driver 1:N Shipment)

scheduledDeparture.description: Planned date and time for shipment departure.

actualDeparture.description: Actual date and time of shipment departure.

estimatedArrival.description: Estimated date and time for shipment arrival.

actualArrival.description: Actual date and time of shipment arrival.

chemicalBatchIds.description: References to ChemicalBatch entities included in this shipment. (Relationship: ChemicalBatch N:N Shipment)

routePath.description: An ordered array of geographical coordinates representing the planned or actual route of the shipment. Each item is a string 'latitude,longitude'.

lastUpdateAt.description: Timestamp when the shipment's data was last updated.

ChemicalBatch.title: ChemicalBatch
ChemicalBatch.description: Represents a specific batch of chemicals, detailing its properties and hazard information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChemicalBatch entity.

name.description: The common name or identifier for the chemical batch (e.g., 'Sulfuric Acid Batch A123').

chemicalType.description: The type or category of the chemical (e.g., 'Corrosive', 'Flammable', 'Toxic').

quantity.description: The quantity of the chemical in this batch.

unit.description: The unit of measurement for the quantity (e.g., 'liters', 'kg', 'gallons').

productionDate.description: Date when the chemical batch was produced.

expirationDate.description: Date when the chemical batch expires.

hazmatClassification.description: Hazardous materials classification for the batch (e.g., 'UN1830').

storageConditions.description: Required storage conditions for the chemical batch (e.g., 'Cool, dry place').

IoTDevice.title: IoTDevice
IoTDevice.description: Represents an Internet of Things device (sensor, GPS unit, camera) deployed for monitoring purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the IoTDevice entity.

serialNumber.description: The unique serial number of the IoT device.

type.description: The type of sensor or device (e.g., 'GPS', 'Temperature Sensor', 'Tamper Sensor', 'Camera').

status.description: Current operational status of the device (e.g., 'Online', 'Offline', 'Error', 'Low Battery').

assignedToVehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle entity to which this device is assigned. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N IoTDevice)

lastReportedAt.description: Timestamp when the device last reported data.

currentLocation.description: Current geographical coordinates reported by the device (e.g., 'latitude,longitude').

TransportAlert.title: TransportAlert
TransportAlert.description: Represents an alert generated due to an anomaly or incident related to a shipment or vehicle.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TransportAlert entity.

shipmentId.description: Reference to the Shipment entity related to this alert. (Relationship: Shipment 1:N TransportAlert)

vehicleId.description: Reference to the Vehicle entity related to this alert. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N TransportAlert)

type.description: The type of alert (e.g., 'Temperature Excursion', 'Route Deviation', 'Tamper Detected', 'Geo-fence Breach', 'Vehicle Malfunction').

severity.description: The severity level of the alert (e.g., 'Critical', 'Warning', 'Info').

timestamp.description: Timestamp when the alert was generated.

location.description: Geographical coordinates where the alert originated (e.g., 'latitude,longitude').

locationDescription.description: A human-readable description of the alert's location.

status.description: The current resolution status of the alert (e.g., 'New', 'Acknowledged', 'Resolved', 'False Positive').

description.description: A detailed description of the alert incident.

assignedToUserId.description: Reference to the User entity who is assigned to resolve this alert. (Relationship: User 1:N TransportAlert)

ComplianceRecord.title: ComplianceRecord
ComplianceRecord.description: Records details about compliance checks, audits, or regulatory adherence for shipments or vehicles.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ComplianceRecord entity.

type.description: The type of compliance record (e.g., 'Safety Audit', 'Regulatory Inspection', 'Internal Check').

dateRecorded.description: The date when the compliance record was created or the event occurred.

score.description: A numerical score indicating the level of compliance, if applicable (e.g., 0-100).

status.description: The overall status of compliance (e.g., 'Compliant', 'Non-Compliant', 'Pending Review').

details.description: Detailed notes or findings from the compliance check.

relatedShipmentId.description: Reference to a Shipment entity if this record is shipment-specific. (Relationship: Shipment 1:N ComplianceRecord)

relatedVehicleId.description: Reference to a Vehicle entity if this record is vehicle-specific. (Relationship: Vehicle 1:N ComplianceRecord)

auditedByUserId.description: Reference to the User who conducted or recorded the compliance check. (Relationship: User 1:N ComplianceRecord)

definition.description: Stores administrative user profiles and their assigned roles for Dashboard Based Access Control (DBAC). Users can manage their own profile, and higher roles can manage others. Includes 'role' field for authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user.

definition.description: Master data collection for all registered drivers. Read-only for general dashboard monitoring, write access restricted by user roles.

params.name: driverId
params.description: The unique identifier of the driver.

definition.description: Master data collection for all defined locations (origins, destinations, checkpoints). Read-only for general dashboard monitoring, write access restricted by user roles.

params.name: locationId
params.description: The unique identifier of the location.

definition.description: Contains real-time and static data for all transport vehicles. Used for 'Vehicles Online' and 'Vehicle Status Indicators'. Includes denormalized 'driverId' for direct linkage.

params.name: vehicleId
params.description: The unique identifier of the vehicle.

definition.description: Stores details for all shipments, including status, current location, and route. Used for 'Active Shipments', 'Live GPS Shipment Map', and 'Shipment Activity Tracking'. Includes denormalized 'vehicleId' and 'driverId' for direct access to related entities without requiring 'get()' calls.

params.name: shipmentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the shipment.

definition.description: Master data collection for all chemical batches. Read-only for general dashboard monitoring, write access restricted by user roles.

params.name: batchId
params.description: The unique identifier of the chemical batch.

definition.description: Stores status and details for all IoT devices. Used for 'Device Status Indicators'. Includes denormalized 'assignedToVehicleId' for direct linkage.

params.name: deviceId
params.description: The unique identifier of the IoT device.

definition.description: Records all generated alerts related to shipments or vehicles. Used for 'Critical Alerts' and 'Recent Alerts Table'. Includes denormalized 'shipmentId', 'vehicleId', and 'assignedToUserId' for authorization independence and direct query capabilities.

params.name: alertId
params.description: The unique identifier of the transport alert.

definition.description: Stores records of compliance checks and audits. Used for 'Compliance Score'. Includes denormalized 'relatedShipmentId', 'relatedVehicleId', and 'auditedByUserId' for authorization independence and direct query capabilities.

params.name: recordId
params.description: The unique identifier of the compliance record.

firestore.reasoning: The Firestore data structure for the ChemGuard AI Transport Portal dashboard prioritizes flat, top-level collections for primary entities like 'shipments', 'vehicles', and 'transport_alerts'. This approach ensures optimal performance for real-time dashboards and simplifies security rules by adhering to the principle of structural segregation, where each collection maintains a homogeneous security posture. Authorization Independence is primarily achieved through: 1. User Role Denormalization: User roles are stored directly within each user's document in the /users/{userId} collection. This allows security rules to evaluate request.auth.uid against a user's role without requiring complex cross-document reads (get()) when authorizing actions on global data. For instance, a rule might verify get(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(request.auth.uid)).data.role == 'Admin' for write access to sensitive collections. 2. Embedded IDs and References: For relationships, relevant IDs (e.g., driverId in a Vehicle document, vehicleId and driverId in a Shipment document, shipmentId, vehicleId, assignedToUserId in a TransportAlert document) are directly embedded. While these are primarily for data linkage, they indirectly support authorization independence by making all necessary context available within the document itself, reducing the need for chained get() operations if a rule needed to check related entity properties (though in this design, most checks will be role-based for global data). For TransportAlert and ComplianceRecord, the assignedToUserId and auditedByUserId fields are critical for allowing users to access items directly related to them without a get() call to a parent collection. Rules can directly check resource.data.assignedToUserId == request.auth.uid. QAPs (Queries as Authorization Policies) are supported by: 1. Structural Segregation: By placing all 'Active Shipments', 'Vehicles Online', 'Critical Alerts', and 'Compliance Scores' into their respective top-level collections (/shipments, /vehicles, /transport_alerts, /compliance_records), security rules can grant broad read access to authenticated/authorized dashboard users for the entire collection. This means client-side queries can efficiently filter data (e.g., shipments.where('status', '==', 'In Transit')) without the security rules attempting to re-filter or restrict the results based on content, which is inefficient and insecure. The rule simply states if a user *can* read from shipments at all. 2. Explicit Data Fields for Filtering: Data fields like status, healthStatus, severity, movementStatus, and assignedToUserId are consistently used for filtering and querying. For example, fetching 'Critical Alerts' involves a query like collection('transport_alerts').where('severity', '==', 'Critical'). The security rule simply verifies that the requesting user has read access to the transport_alerts collection based on their role in /users/{userId}. This makes queries predictable and performant, as security rules are not used as filters. Overall, the design promotes a secure, scalable, and debuggable system by adhering to the core principles, especially authorization independence and QAPs, through thoughtful collection segregation and denormalization of key authorization fields.
